note: This flare originated from beyond the western limb, likely associated with former Active Region 14307 which rotated off of the Earth-facing disk on 2025-12-19.
There may be a sympathetic flare from Active Region 14324 as well.
This flare originates from beyond the east limb as seen from SDO AIA imagery and is likely occulted. Active Region 14341 was numbered on 2026-01-14.

Occulted flare from behind the W limb as seen from SDO AIA 131. The flare and associated eruption are seen in STEREO A EUV imagery at 2026-01-31T04:49Z, located in former Active Region 4351 (S04W55 as seen from STEREO A).
Peak occurs at 2026-02-02T20:09Z as it wavers ~M2.9 for several minutes.
Double peaked flare with following M1.2 peak at 2026-02-05T08:46Z.
Double peaked flare with preceeding M1.0 peak at 2026-02-05T08:33Z.
Simultaneous, but more minor, flaring is observed from Active Region 14366 (N14W16) at this time.
Double peaked flare followed by M1.3 peaking at 2026-02-05T16:39Z.
Double peaked flare preceeded by M1.6 peaking at 2026-02-05T16:30Z.
Occulted flare from beyond the E limb
Associated with the Type II radio emission.
This flare originated from an unnumbered region at the time of this event which has since been numbered as Active Region 14380.
Occulted flare on NE limb from the newly numbered AR 14384 rotating on disk, so the location of the AR/flare is somewhat uncertain.
